text:
Spectators watching the BBC's coverage of the Olympic Games 2024 couldn't believe their eyes as the programme was hilariously interrupted by window cleaners.

Those tuned in for the Paris 2024 excitement on Saturday saw presenter Hazel Irvine and diving expert Tonia Couch discussing the events when suddenly two men appeared outside.

Quick on her feet, Hazel made light of the surprise cameo, joking: "Can you see this behind me? Because we've got two gentlemen fixing our studio. I don't think they know they're on telly at the moment," as reported by the Mirror.

Oblivious to their newfound fame, the cleaners continued with their task, inadvertently stealing the spotlight and prompting viewers to take to Twitter to express their delight. The impromptu appearance sparked a flurry of tweets as the window-cleaning duo became an instant hit.

One entertained viewer tweeted: "Live Olympic window cleaning on the BBC!" while another added: "Window cleaners have just turned up on the BBC coverage."

A third commented: "Just a casual bit of window cleaning in the middle of the BBC coverage." Another noted: "The rain is still TIPPING down in Paris. And now the BBC has had to get the window cleaners in whilst they're on air."

And yet another Twitter user joked: "I didn't know window cleaning now qualified as an Olympic sport."

GB News presenter Martin Daubney faced another blunder this week, following an awkward start to his news hour.

In a decision that failed to win over viewers, BBC producers chose to use green screen technology to craft a backdrop for an early morning chat show delivered by Jeanette Kwakye, with guests including JJ Chalmers and Fred Sirieix.

Confusingly, a digitally-constructed city courtyard was used as the setting for their discussion. Fans were quick to voice their disappointment with the arrangement, given that the show is filmed in central Paris in a studio that boasts views of the iconic Eiffel Tower.

One annoyed viewer took to the internet to air their grievances, posting: "This green screen stuff is horrific. Stop it. You've got a studio in Paris looking at the Eiffel Tower ffs." (sic).

title: BBC presenter halts Olympic coverage after broadcast interrupted by cleaners - Daily Record
author: Abbie Bray
description: BBC viewers were left in stitches when a pair of window cleaners unexpectedly appeared during the live broadcast of the 2024 Olympics in Paris.
